title: Helietta mollis
intro: Helietta mollis, also known as the smooth helietta, is a plant species within the Rutaceae family, native to the tropical regions of South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, boasting various health benefits, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which aid in treating wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Helietta mollis include limonoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Helietta mollis is commonly prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures to treat a range of health conditions, making it a valuable plant in folk medicine.
taxonomy: Helietta mollis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It belongs to the genus Helietta, which comprises around 10 species of plants. Helietta mollis is native to South America, specifically in the tropical and subtropical regions of the continent. The plant is a shrub or small tree that produces small white flowers and is often used in traditional medicine. The exact classification of Helietta mollis can vary depending on the source, but it is generally agreed to be a member of the Rutaceae family.
uses: Helietta mollis, also known as the soft helietta, is a plant species native to Brazil and other parts of South America. Its bark and leaves have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and swelling.
